title: Password Requirement
description: Component for displaying live password validation feedback based on defined requirements, optionally with strength progress bars.
summary: Shows live validation feedback for password requirements.

body.raw:
## Usage

Basic usage example to quickly see how the `PasswordRequirement` works.

<PasswordRequirementDemosUsage />

## Properties

Interactive configurator to explore customization options for the `PasswordRequirement` component.

<PasswordRequirementDemosConfigurator />

### Managing State with Provider

When using the `PasswordRequirementProvider` to automatically manage value and onChange state, you must use the `<Input.Password />` component.

```tsx
export function PasswordRequirementDemo() {
  return (
    <PasswordRequirementProvider>
      <Stack>
        <Input.Password />
        <PasswordRequirement />
      </Stack>
    </PasswordRequirementProvider>
  );
}
```

### Controlling State Manually

You can also manually control the input's value and pass it to the provider via value and onValueChange props.

```tsx
export function PasswordRequirementDemo() {
  const [value, setValue] = React.useState<string>("");
  return (
    <PasswordRequirementProvider value={value} onValueChange={setValue}>
      <input value={value} onChange={e => setValue(e.currentTarget.value)} />
      <PasswordRequirement />
    </PasswordRequirementProvider>
  );
}
```

## API References

### Styles API

The `classNames` and `styles` props allow you to customize the appearance of the component parts.

```ts
type Selector = "root" | "item" | "icon" | "label" | "progressRoot" | "progressIndicator";
```

| Styles API    | Type                                       | Default     | Annotation |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------ | ----------- | ---------- |
| classNames`?` | `Partial<Record<Selector, string>>`        | `undefined` | ---        |
| styles`?`     | `Partial<Record<Selector, CSSProperties>>` | `undefined` | ---        |

### Props API

| Props API           | Type                             | Default     | Annotation |
| ------------------- | -------------------------------- | ----------- | ---------- |
| value`?`            | `string`                         | `undefined` | ---        |
| withProgressBars`?` | `boolean`                        | `undefined` | ---        |
| requirements`?`     | `Requirements[]`                 | `undefined` | ---        |
| strength`?`         | `{ min?: number; max?: number }` | `undefined` | ---        |

---

## Source Codes

Full working code example, including necessary markup and styles.
You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the component immediately.

<SourceCodes name="password-requirement" />

`,(0,r.jsx)(t,{name:"password-requirement",code:`"use client";\r
import * as React from "react";\r
import { Svg } from "@/ui/svg";\r
import { cn } from "@/utils/cn";\r
\r
export const requirements = (min: number = 10, max: number = 100) => [\r
  { regex: new RegExp(\`^.{\${min},\${max}}$\`), label: \`Minimum \${min} characters and maximum \${max} characters\` },\r
  { regex: /[a-z]/, label: "Minimum one lowercase character" },\r
  { regex: /[A-Z]/, label: "Minimum one uppercase character" },\r
  { regex: /[0-9]/, label: "Minimum one number" },\r
  { regex: /[$&+,:;=?@#|'<>.^*()%!-]/, label: "At least one special character, e.g, !@#?" }\r
];\r
\r
const meetsSvgMap = {\r
  true: ["M5 12l5 5l10 -10"],\r
  false: ["M12 18a6 6 0 1 0 0-12 6 6 0 0 0 0 12Z"]\r
} as const;\r
\r
function getStrengthPassword(psw: string, min: number, required: readonly { regex: RegExp; label: string }[]): number {\r
  let multiplier = psw.length > min - 1 ? 0 : 1;\r
  required.forEach(rq => {\r
    if (!rq.regex.test(psw)) {\r
      multiplier += 1;\r
    }\r
  });\r
  return Math.max(100 - (100 / (required.length + 1)) * multiplier, 10);\r
}\r
\r
type Selector = "root" | "item" | "icon" | "label" | "progressRoot" | "progressIndicator";\r
interface Requirements {\r
  regex: RegExp;\r
  label: string;\r
}\r
interface CtxProps {\r
  value: string;\r
  onValueChange: (value: SetStateAction<string>) => void;\r
  requirements: Requirements[];\r
  strength: number;\r
  withProgressBars: boolean;\r
}\r
\r
const ctx = React.createContext<CtxProps | undefined>(undefined);\r
export const usePasswordRequirementCtx = () => React.useContext(ctx)!;\r
\r
interface RequirementState {\r
  meets(): {\r
    isPass(): boolean;\r
  }[];\r
}\r
export interface PasswordRequirementProviderProps extends Partial<Omit<CtxProps, "strength">> {\r
  children: React.ReactNode | ((state: RequirementState) => React.ReactNode);\r
  strength?: { min?: number; max?: number };\r
}\r
\r
export function PasswordRequirementProvider(_props: PasswordRequirementProviderProps) {\r
  const { children, value: valueProp, onValueChange: onValueChangeProp, requirements: requirementsProp, strength: strengthProp = {}, withProgressBars = false } = _props;\r
\r
  const { min = 10, max = 100 } = strengthProp;\r
\r
  const [_value, _setValue] = React.useState<string>("");\r
\r
  const onValue = valueProp ?? _value;\r
  const onValueChange = React.useCallback(\r
    (value: SetStateAction<string>) => {\r
      const valueState = typeof value === "function" ? value(onValue) : value;\r
      if (onValueChangeProp) onValueChangeProp(valueState);\r
      else _setValue(valueState);\r
    },\r
    [onValueChangeProp, onValue]\r
  );\r
\r
  const required = requirementsProp ?? requirements(min, max);\r
  const strength = getStrengthPassword(onValue, min, required);\r
  const isMeets = required.map(rq => ({ isPass: () => rq.regex.test(onValue) }));\r
\r
  const contextValue = React.useMemo<CtxProps>(\r
    () => ({\r
      value: onValue,\r
      onValueChange,\r
      requirements: required,\r
      strength,\r
      withProgressBars\r
    }),\r
    [onValue, onValueChange, required, strength, withProgressBars]\r
  );\r
\r
  return (\r
    <ctx.Provider value={contextValue}>\r
      {typeof children === "function"\r
        ? children({\r
            meets() {\r
              return isMeets;\r
            }\r
          })\r
        : children}\r
    </ctx.Provider>\r
  );\r
}\r
\r
interface PasswordRequirementProps extends React.ComponentProps<"ul"> {\r
  value?: string;\r
  withProgressBars?: boolean;\r
  classNames?: Partial<Record<Selector, string>>;\r
  styles?: Partial<Record<Selector, React.CSSProperties & Record<string, any>>>;\r
  style?: React.CSSProperties & Record<string, any>;\r
  requirements?: Requirements[];\r
  strength?: { min?: number; max?: number };\r
}\r
export const PasswordRequirement = React.forwardRef<HTMLUListElement, PasswordRequirementProps>((_props, ref) => {\r
  const { value: valueProp, role = "list", withProgressBars: withProgressBarsProp = false, style, styles, className, classNames, requirements: requirementsProp, strength: strengthProp = {}, ...props } = _props;\r
\r
  const { min = 10, max = 100 } = strengthProp;\r
\r
  const ctx = usePasswordRequirementCtx();\r
  const value = ctx ? ctx.value : (valueProp ?? "");\r
  const withProgressBars = ctx ? ctx.withProgressBars : withProgressBarsProp;\r
  const required = ctx ? ctx.requirements : (requirementsProp ?? requirements(min, max));\r
  const strength = ctx ? ctx.strength : getStrengthPassword(value, min, required);\r
\r
  const rqLength = required.length;\r
\r
  const progressBars = Array(rqLength)\r
    .fill(0)\r
    .map((_, index) => {\r
      const position = value.length > 0 && index === 0 ? 100 : strength >= ((index + 1) / rqLength) * 100 ? 100 : 0;\r
      return (\r
        <div\r
          key={index}\r
          {...{\r
            "aria-valuemax": 100,\r
            "aria-valuemin": 0,\r
            "data-state": "indeterminate",\r
            "data-max": "100",\r
            "aria-label": \`rqpsw-progress-\${index + 1}\`,\r
            role: "progressbar",\r
            className: cn("relative flex h-[10px] w-full overflow-hidden rounded-full border", classNames?.progressRoot),\r
            style: styles?.progressRoot\r
          }}\r
        >\r
          <div\r
            {...{\r
              "data-state": "indeterminate",\r
              "data-max": "100",\r
              className: cn("h-full w-full flex-1 transition-all", strength > 90 ? "bg-[#22c55e]" : strength > 50 ? "bg-[#ca8a04]" : "bg-[#dc2626]", classNames?.progressIndicator),\r
              style: { ...styles?.progressIndicator, transform: \`translateX(-\${100 - position}%)\` }\r
            }}\r
          />\r
        </div>\r
      );\r
    });\r
\r
  return (\r
    <ul ref={ref} className={cn("flex w-full flex-col text-[13px] md:col-span-2 md:text-sm", className, classNames?.root)} {...{ role, style: { ...style, ...styles?.root }, ...props }}>\r
      {withProgressBars && (\r
        <li role="listitem" className={cn("my-2 grid w-full grid-cols-5 gap-2", classNames?.item)} {...{ style: styles?.item }}>\r
          {progressBars}\r
        </li>\r
      )}\r
      {required.map((rq, index) => {\r
        const meets = rq.regex.test(value);\r
        return (\r
          <li key={index} role="listitem" data-state={meets ? "pass" : undefined} className={cn("relative flex w-full flex-row flex-nowrap items-start gap-1.5 truncate text-wrap font-normal text-color data-[state=pass]:text-[#22c55e]", classNames?.item)} {...{ style: styles?.item }}>\r
            <Svg size={12} className={cn("relative top-1", classNames?.icon)} {...{ style: styles?.icon }}>\r
              {meetsSvgMap[String(meets) as keyof typeof meetsSvgMap].map(i => (\r
                <path key={i} d={i} />\r
              ))}\r
            </Svg>\r
            <p data-state={meets ? "pass" : undefined} className={classNames?.label} {...{ style: styles?.label }}>\r
              {rq.label}\r
            </p>\r
          </li>\r
        );\r
      })}\r
    </ul>\r
  );\r
});\r
PasswordRequirement.displayName = "PasswordRequirement";`,ext:".tsx",repo:"web/components/password-requirement.tsx"})]})}function p(n={}){let{wrapper:e}=n.components||{};return e?(0,r.jsx)(e,{...n,children:(0,r.jsx)(h,{...n})}):h(n)}function o(n,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+n+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return P(k);})();
